qa_pairs.question: What is the specific Slurm configuration for running a GROMACS simulation on a whole node on the Narval cluster, according to the provided job script?
qa_pairs.answer: For a GROMACS simulation on a Narval whole node, the Slurm job script requests one node (`#SBATCH --nodes=1`), 32 MPI tasks per node (`#SBATCH --ntasks-per-node=32`), and 2 OpenMP threads per MPI task (`#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=2`). This configuration totals 64 CPUs per node, allocates 2000 MB of memory per CPU (`#SBATCH --mem-per-cpu=2000M`), and sets a time limit of 1 hour (`#SBATCH --time=0-01:00`).

qa_pairs.question: Which modules are required to run GROMACS version 2025.4 on a whole compute node using the example Slurm scripts provided?
qa_pairs.answer: To run GROMACS version 2025.4 on a whole compute node using the provided example Slurm scripts, you need to load `StdEnv/2023`, `gcc/12.3`, `openmpi/4.1.5`, and `gromacs/2025.4`.
